mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in residential and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a monthly decline in industrial production signals supply-side contraction that immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when GDP expands.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in >65% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 210.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls signals severe labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to generate a statistically significant negative return differential (p > 0.05) over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly retail sales decline exceeding 2.0% signals an abrupt consumer spending shock that immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Retail sales drops above 2.0% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
